result:
Build is green. Let me write a brief report.

**Root cause**: `crates/lua-stdlib/src/os_lib.rs:344-352` — `os_tmpname()` was a Phase-A stub that always returned `LuaError::runtime("unable to generate a unique filename")`. `files.lua` calls `os.tmpname()` on line ~49 to obtain a path before opening a temp file, so execution aborted immediately.

**Fix**: Implemented `os_tmpname` to compose a unique path from `std::env::temp_dir()` + `SystemTime` nanoseconds + an `AtomicU64` counter, formatted as `lua_{nanos:x}_{counter:x}`. This matches the `tmpnam` (ISO-C) branch of the reference (`reference/lua-5.4.7/src/loslib.c:124`) — name-only generation; the caller's `io.open(file, "w")` would create the file. `std::env` is already permitted in lua-stdlib (see `loadlib.rs:20`).

**State after fix**: `cargo build -p lua-cli` is green. I did not re-run the test (per instructions: "Do NOT run the program at the end — the mega-loop will re-test"). `files.lua` is a heavyweight file-I/O test and will likely fail next on `io.open` (still stubbed in `io_lib.rs:453`), but that's a separate bug — the specific `os.tmpname` failure is resolved.
